# Global Allocation Registry

## Purpose

Tracks cross-page usage of differentiators, section archetypes, audience fears, and FAQ themes. Each wireframe subagent reads this before starting. The main agent updates it after each wireframe batch completes.

---

## Differentiator Prominence by Page

| Differentiator | Featured Prominently On | Brief Reference On | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Principal-led work, not delegated | About, Discovery | Option A, Option B | Named principals (James, Andy) appear across all pages in header/footer, but About establishes credibility depth |
| Conversion-engineered architecture | Option B, Homepage | Option A, Blog Posts | Option B is where full technical build is explained; Homepage shows the concept at high level |
| Dual-channel semantic structure | Option A, Blog Posts | Discovery, Option B | Option A focuses on AI/search visibility as core benefit; Blog Posts demonstrate the thinking |
| AI visibility baseline & monitoring | Discovery, Ongoing | Option A, Option B | Discovery introduces the audit; Ongoing explains the monitoring layer |
| CMS designed for non-technical teams | Option B | Option A, Ongoing | Option B is where implementation is discussed; light references in others |
| 100% client ownership | Option A, Option B, Contact | Discovery, Ongoing | Both service options emphasize ownership; Contact reinforces in final commitment step |
| Specific off-site guidance without upsell | About, Blog Posts | Discovery, Services | About establishes the philosophy; Blog Posts demonstrate honest thinking without execution upsell |
| Discovery focus on business positioning | Discovery, Homepage | Option A, Option B | Discovery page explains the diagnostic; Homepage implies it through the qualification section |

---

## Section Archetype Usage

| Archetype/Pattern | Used On | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Hero + Value Statement + Primary CTA | All pages | Consistent header structure |
| Rapid Qualification / Scenario Matching | Homepage | Routes visitors by persona/situation |
| Principal Bio + Credentials | About | Establishes authority and removes trust barriers |
| "This Is For You If" Scenarios | Workshop, Option A, Option B | Clarifies fit before deep engagement |
| What You'll Get / Deliverables | Workshop, Discovery, Option A, Option B | Concrete outputs build confidence |
| Process/Timeline | Discovery, Option A, Option B, Ongoing | Shows what actually happens and when |
| FAQ Section | Discovery, Option B | Addresses specific objections and investment questions |
| Success Indicators / Results Timeline | Ongoing, Option A, Option B | Shows when and how the business will see impact |
| Related/Next Step Links | Blog Hub, Blog Posts | Connects content network |
| Trust Signals Section | About | Social proof and past client types (without naming) |
| Alternative Service Links | Option A ↔ Option B, Ongoing as next step for both | Routes visitors to adjacent services |
| Off-Site Recommendations Summary | About, Option A | Demonstrates holistic thinking |

---

## Audience Fear Coverage

| Fear/Pain Point | Agitated On (Primary) | Referenced On (Light) | Agitation Approach | Agitation Depth |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Website is outdated and holding the business back | Option B, Homepage | About, Blog Posts | Platform constraints block growth. You can't optimize your way out of a broken foundation. | Medium |
| Positioning is vague/generic; real expertise isn't reflected | Option A, Homepage | Workshop, Blog Posts | Gap between what you are and what your website says. Content is thin. Machines can't identify who you are. | Medium |
| Traffic is softening and I don't know why | Homepage, Blog Hub | Blog Posts, Workshop | AI is changing discovery. You're invisible to new search channels. Competitors are pulling ahead digitally. | Light to Medium |
| Previous agency work wasted money or damaged reputation | About, Workshop | Discovery | Opaque retainers with no results. Monthly activity reports masking zero impact. No owned assets. | Medium (for Burned Decision Maker) |
| Business reputation is at risk; I need accuracy and control | About, Contact | Option A | Risky external tactics. Misinformation spreading. Loss of professional standing. | Medium (for Medical/YMYL) |
| I don't know what's actually needed; I'm uncertain | Workshop, Homepage | Blog Hub | Self-assessment lowers barrier. Free value upfront. We're here to help you see clearly before you commit. | Light |
| AI is changing how customers find me, but I don't understand it | Blog Hub, Blog Posts | Homepage, Discovery | Your competitors are becoming discoverable and citable in ChatGPT. Your business isn't. This is happening now. | Light to Medium |
| I want ownership, not another monthly retainer I can't control | Option A, Option B, Contact | About, Discovery | 100% client ownership. Nothing disappears when the contract ends. You know exactly what's being built. | Medium |

---

## FAQ Theme Allocation

| Question Theme | Covered On | Do NOT Repeat On | Agitation/Answer Approach |
|---|---|---|---|
| "What exactly will I get?" / Deliverables clarity | Discovery, Option A, Option B | Workshop, Ongoing | Specific outputs listed (e.g., "You'll receive a written strategic report with three recommended paths"). Ownership emphasized. |
| "How long does this take?" / Timeline | Discovery, Option A, Option B, Ongoing | Workshop | Realistic timelines given per phase. Set expectations upfront. |
| "What if I'm not ready to build a full website?" | Discovery, Option A | None | Two clear paths: positioning work alone (Option A) or full build (Option B). Choice respects budget and readiness. |
| "How much does it cost?" / Investment | Discovery, Option A, Option B, Ongoing | Homepage, Workshop | Transparency on investment level (e.g., "Discovery: NZ$X,XXX"). "Why paid? Real work deserves real investment." |
| "What makes you different from other agencies?" | About, Option B | None | Principal-led (not delegated). Business strategy first. Client ownership. Specific methodology. |
| "Will you help with off-site things like PR or reviews?" | About, Option A | Discovery, Option B (light reference) | Yes, with honest guidance. No execution upsell because we don't sell those services. |
| "Can I do this myself?" / Self-service option | Workshop, Blog Hub | Option A, Option B | Free workshop available. Blog has free insights. But strategic work has real value; workshop outputs guide next steps. |
| "What if your approach isn't right for us?" | Contact, Discovery | None | Fit conversation is two-way. We'll be direct. Not every business is a good fit. |
| "What happens after discovery?" / Path forward | Discovery | Option A, Option B (but they should reference discovery as optional pre-service step) | Discovery findings become brief for whichever service option the client chooses. Clear next steps. |
| "How does ongoing work differ from one-time projects?" | Ongoing | None | Ongoing builds compounding asset, not production volume. Strategically directed content. Client-owned throughout. |
| "What if I already have some of this work done?" | Discovery (implicit), Option A | None | Discovery audit reviews current state and recommends what's valuable to keep vs. redo. |
| "Is my business the right size/type for WebXC?" | Discovery, Option A, Option B | Homepage (light: qualification scenario) | For whom section on each service describes ideal fit. Discovery helps clarify. No universal one-size-fits-all. |

---

## Interactive Element Usage

| Element Type | Used On | Alternative for Next Use |
|---|---|---|
| Scenario matching / rapid qualification | Homepage | Case study selector or timeline slider (for future content hub) |
| Self-assessment worksheet (downloadable or embedded) | Workshop | Interactive tool or form-based assessment (if expanding to multiple diagnostic formats) |
| Accordion/expandable FAQ | Discovery, Option B | Tabbed content or progressive disclosure (if multiple FAQs emerge) |
| Timeline/process visualization | Option A, Option B, Ongoing | Step-by-step guided tour or animation |
| Comparison table (Option A vs. Option B) | Services hub (if created) or within service pages as sidebars | Feature matrix or interactive comparison tool |
| Testimonial/case metric carousel | Homepage (if social proof is added) | Video testimonial player or detailed case study library |

---

## Rules

1. **Differentiator Prominence:** Each major differentiator should be "featured prominently" on ONE page only. Brief references (1-2 words, no explanation) are acceptable on other pages.

2. **Fear Coverage:** Each page must use a DIFFERENT primary audience fear for its Problem Agitation section. The Burned Decision Maker's fear (previous agency waste) agitates on About and Workshop. The Legacy Moderniser's fear (platform constraints) agitates on Option B. The Strategically Unaware Leader's fear (AI visibility gap) agitates on Blog Hub. The Medical/YMYL Specialist's fear (reputation risk) agitates on About and Contact. No fear is used identically across pages.

3. **FAQ Themes:** FAQ themes covered in depth on one page should NOT be repeated identically on other pages. Instead, link from the page with the full answer to the page with the quick reference. Example: If Discovery fully explains "What makes you different?" link from Option A to that Discovery FAQ rather than repeating.

4. **Interactive Elements:** Element types should vary across pages where possible to maintain visual and behavioral interest. Don't use the same interactive pattern on consecutive pages.

5. **Section Archetypes:** Section archetypes should not repeat on consecutive pages. If Homepage uses "Hero + Qualification Scenarios," About should use "Hero + Principal Bios." If Option A uses "This Is For You If," Option B should use a different clarification pattern (e.g., "Your Current Reality").

6. **Agitation Depth:** Agitation depth must match page type. Tier 1 (Homepage) uses light to medium agitation. Tier 2 (About, Workshop, Blog Hub) uses light to medium. Tier 3 (Discovery, Services, Blog Posts) use medium. Tier 4 (Blog Post details) use light to medium. Contact page uses minimal agitation (focus is conversion, not friction).

7. **Persona Routing:** Different personas should land on different page types first. Homepage routes by scenario (Legacy Moderniser → About → Option B; Burned Decision Maker → Workshop → Discovery). Blog Hub attracts Strategically Unaware Leaders. Medical/YMYL Specialists land on About (trust-focused). Every persona has a clear entry point that matches their awareness level.

8. **Content Density:** No page should attempt to cover all value themes. Homepage covers all four lightly (to route correctly). About covers Business-First and Owned Asset prominently. Option A covers Conversion & Performance Machine and AI & Search Readiness. Option B covers Business-First Build and Conversion Machine. Ongoing covers Authority Maintenance. Blog Hub and Posts establish thought leadership and free value.

---

## Update Log

**Status:** Initialized. Awaiting first wireframe batch completion.

After each wireframe batch completes, this section will be updated with:
- Date
- Pages completed
- Differentiators featured prominently
- Fears agitated on each page
- FAQ themes covered
- Interactive elements used
- Any adjustments to rules or allocations based on designer/copywriter feedback
